title: olea aquifolium var. cuneata
intro: Olea aquifolium var. cuneata, also known as the holly-leaved olive, is a shrubby evergreen plant native to Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The holly-leaved olive is said to have various health benefits, such as relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and treating skin conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to extract its medicinal properties.
